title: Add event-specific tyre-rule pressure features
summary: Public F1 strategy is not governed only by generic compound life.
rationale: Public F1 strategy is not governed only by generic compound life. FIA/F1/Pirelli rules create hard stop-pressure spikes: normal dry races require two dry specifications unless wet tyres are used, 2025 Monaco required three tyre sets, and Qatar had maximum set-life limits in 2023 and 2025.
implementation_hint: Add Race_Year flags and rule-derived counters: required_remaining_stops_by_rule, laps_to_Qatar_stint_cap, Monaco_three_set_debt, wet_rule_exemption_seen, and interactions with TyreLife/Stint/PriorPitCount. Keep rules as features or small post-rank boosts, not hard labels, unless train validation proves deterministic.
expected_effect: Should improve rare but high-signal race-year rows where static compound windows understate mandatory stop urgency.
risk: Sparse events can overfit local CV; race names may omit Qatar/Monaco or synthetic data may not follow real regulations exactly.
